meta.title: Per-day route metrics & ideas
meta.disclaimer: Distances from Google Directions (driving, metric): each day sums all legs in that link; the full-route row sums those days. No live traffic—confirm in Maps before riding.

1.distanceNote: Driving km come from the Google Maps API for the same origin/destination as the link. Your chosen border crossing and pins can still change the route — confirm in Maps before you ride.

recommendations.text: Pre-clear eTA/ESTA not needed for US land — have passport, reg, insurance ready.
Optional break: Niagara Falls NY state park (goat island) if crossing near Niagara.
Watkins Glen State Park gorge (short hike) if you arrive with daylight.

2.distanceNote: Finger Lakes to NYC metro — pick Southampton vs DC pacing in Maps after you choose host; tolls on Thruway/I-95 possible.

recommendations.text: If staying Southampton: adjust destination pin in Google Maps.
George Washington Bridge / Hudson crossings — peak traffic.

3.distanceNote: Full day as titled: NYC metro → DC → Luray. If you actually leave from Long Island (Day 2), change origin in Maps to your Southampton pin — distances will shorten slightly vs this NYC centroid.

recommendations.text: Shenandoah approaches — Skyline Dr is a detour with fee.
I-95 / Beltway congestion — avoid rush if possible.

4.distanceNote: ~150–180 km depending on I-81 vs US-460 routing — confirm scenic choice in Maps.

recommendations.text: Shenandoah NP Skyline Dr is a fee detour — optional.
Roanoke: Blue Ridge Parkway access points south of town.

5.distanceNote: BRP + connectors — Maps may suggest I-81; drag onto Blue Ridge Parkway and secondary roads. Full day at parkway speeds.

recommendations.text: Blue Ridge Parkway — no trucks; plan fuel gaps.
In Maps: use ‘Avoid highways’ or add BRP waypoints to keep off interstates.
Boone NC: food/fuel before final push to Bristol.

6.distanceNote: Smokies gateway + US-129 Dragon + run to Kingston — long, technical day; verify GSMNP entry needs.

recommendations.text: Tail of the Dragon — fuel at Deals Gap.
GSMNP vehicle reservation (peak) — check NPS.

7.distanceNote: ~100–130 km short day — recovery before I-40 west.

recommendations.text: Parsons — resupply before Arkansas leg tomorrow.

8.distanceNote: I-40 W across Mississippi River bridges.

recommendations.text: Memphis river crossing — plan fuel before/after bridge congestion.
Hot Springs NP detour (+~1 h) if staying with Garland County host instead.

9.distanceNote: I-30 W into DFW; summer heat + storms.

recommendations.text: Texas speed limits high — watch fuel range and wind on plains.
DFW traffic peaks: avoid I-35E core at rush if possible.
Cadillac Ranch Amarillo — quick 66 icon near I-40.
Palo Duro Canyon SP if you can spare half-day north of route.

11.distanceNote: Classic I-40 W ~285 mi; matches published Amarillo–Albuquerque driving guides.

recommendations.text: Route 66 Midpoint Adrian — you may have stayed night prior; still good photo stop if passing.
Cadillac Ranch if you skipped Day 9 (Fort Worth).

12.distanceNote: Often US-550 N via Durango area; mountain weather.

recommendations.text: Mesa Verde NP detour (west) if you have +1 day later — not same-day with Silverton push.
Durango fuel/food before climbing toward Silverton.

13.distanceNote: Four Corners + US-491/160; Silverton is north of Durango on 550.

recommendations.text: Million Dollar Highway (US-550) weather — check COtrip / closures.
Monument Valley (AZ) short detour south of Mexican Hat if time/light.

14.distanceNote: Includes Page/Horseshoe/Antelope corridor; long day — your ~500 km plan is realistic.

recommendations.text: Antelope Canyon — book Navajo tour ahead.
Glen Canyon / Lake Powell NRA — Lone Rock beach area if cooling off.
Zion is a major detour W — usually not same-day with Bryce + Page.

15.distanceNote: Travelmath/Arches–Bryce corridor often ~440–450 km depending on UT-12 vs I-70 routing.

recommendations.text: Capitol Reef (UT-12) — scenic buffer; adds time.
Gas in Hanksville or Torrey if taking scenic route — long empty spans.

16.distanceNote: Local Moab-area loop for map + weather (Moab → Arches → Dead Horse Point). Not a touring day — use for pins and 10-day forecast; actual mileage is short.

recommendations.text: Arches NP: verify current entry rules on nps.gov/arch (timed-entry reservations were discontinued as of 2026 research — parking can still cap access when lots are full).
Dead Horse Point State Park — book day-use entry at Utah State Parks (stateparks.utah.gov).
Canyonlands NP Island in the Sky — optional add-on, ~30 min from Dead Horse Point.
Moab town resupply: gear shops, laundry, bike wash, grocery (City Market on Main St).
Start Delicate Arch hike by 7 am — 3.2 mi round trip, 480 ft gain. Full sun and exposed by 10 am.

17.distanceNote: Often ~310 mi via I-70 E to I-15 S — remote I-70 Salina–Green River segment; plan fuel.

recommendations.text: Arches NP: confirm hours and any temporary entry controls on nps.gov/arch before you ride.
Dead Horse Point SP overlook near Moab if skipping Arches repeat.

18.distanceNote: I-15 S Mojave; summer heat extreme.

recommendations.text: Zion NP (Springdale) detour if you skipped earlier — tunnel rules for wide bars.
Valley of Fire NV state park — short detour off I-15.

19.distanceNote: Barstow → LA basin → Leo Carrillo (PCH/Malibu) → Big Sur → Prewitt Ridge. ~590 km total — longest California day. CA-14 into LA, then PCH north. Slow curves on Hwy 1 Big Sur add time beyond distance.

recommendations.text: Depart Barstow by 6:30 am — miss most LA freeway traffic; PCH before 10 am is much cleaner.
Leo Carrillo State Park — day use parking. Spend 2-3 hours at the beach; this is your Pacific arrival moment.
Fuel stop: Cambria / San Simeon — last reliable gas before long Big Sur stretch.
McWay Falls / Julia Pfeiffer Burns SP — short walk to waterfall into ocean.
Bixby Creek Bridge — pull over on north side for arch-over-coast photo.
Nacimiento-Fergusson Rd to Fort Hunter Liggett — ADV detour only (rough). Skip if fatigued on a long day.
California Aqueduct vista along CA-14 — optional industrial landmark before hitting the coast.

20.distanceNote: Coast + inland cutoffs possible in bad weather.

recommendations.text: Bixby Creek Bridge — pullout etiquette.
Mendocino village — coffee if fog lifts.
Avenue of the Giants detour (Humboldt) — worth it.
Elk herds on 101 — dawn/dusk.

22.distanceNote: Long day US-199 + I-5 + OR-126; ~365 mi class routing.

recommendations.text: Crater Lake NP detour adds significant miles — usually a separate day.
Oregon Caves NM (OR-46) if you love twisty side roads.

23.distanceNote: Cascade routing via Portland/Vancouver or eastern OR depending on choice.

recommendations.text: Columbia River Gorge I-84 — wind + vista stops (Multnomah reservations peak season).
Mosier (Rowena Crest) if heading to Gorge hosts.

24.distanceNote: I-84 to I-5 N ~192 mi core; Seattle metro adds time.

recommendations.text: Mount St. Helens Johnston Ridge (detour) — long day add-on.
Seattle HOV rules / bridge tolls — check WSDOT.

25.distanceNote: WA SR-20 North Cascades — seasonal opening critical (snow). ~180 mi.

recommendations.text: North Cascades Hwy opening dates — snow closures.
If SR-20 closed: reroute via I-90 to Wenatchee (+ km).
Lake Chelan detour (south) if you want water break.
Grand Coulee Dam visitor area (north detour option).

27.distanceNote: Matches ~207 mi Spokane–Lolo area references on US-12 corridor.

recommendations.text: US-12 Lolo Pass — curves next 99 miles; deer/elk.
Clearwater River overlooks — fuel in Kooskia/Lowell.

28.distanceNote: ~Lolo to Bozeman ~213 mi + south to gateway; verify your actual start pin at pass vs Lolo town.

recommendations.text: Yellowstone west entrance at West Yellowstone — expect summer queues.
Grizzly & Wolf Discovery Center if staying West Yellowstone.

29.distanceNote: In-park miles vary wildly by loop; figure is a moderate geyser-basin day, not perimeter.

recommendations.text: NPS Trip Planner + road construction.
Bison jams — never crowd wildlife; pass buying online.

30.distanceNote: ~316 km (typical): Cooke City, MT (Yellowstone NE gate) → Red Lodge via US-212 Beartooth → Powell, WY. This is the Beartooth+Bighorn link the app totals — cross-park miles from Old Faithful / Canyon to Cooke are extra (~120–180 km); plan that leg separately.

recommendations.text: Beartooth Hwy (US-212) status — snow/weather.
If Maps suggests WY-310 toward Cody — override: stay on US-212 from Cooke City through Red Lodge for the pass.
Cody Buffalo Bill Center — optional only if you detour; not on the 212 Beartooth line.

31.distanceNote: Powell → Gillette: Bighorn Basin / I-90 style routing; often ~300–340 km depending on corridor.

recommendations.text: Bighorn Canyon NRA (optional north detour).
Wind River Canyon (US-20) if routing that way — scenic.

32.distanceNote: Black Hills approach; Sturgis week traffic if August.

recommendations.text: Needles Highway / Iron Mountain Rd — tight tunnels; check vehicle height.
Mount Rushmore — evening lighting if time.

33.distanceNote: Matches your ~600 km slab day; I-90 E ~370 mi.

recommendations.text: Fuel every half tank — prairie crosswinds.
Badlands NP / Wall Drug — detour south if +half day.

34.distanceNote: I-90 E to Madison/Milwaukee arc ~277 mi.

recommendations.text: Wisconsin Dells detour — touristy but easy off I-90.

35.distanceNote: Oconomowoc → Benton Harbor/Coloma area (~142 mi) + Benton Harbor → Port Huron → Sarnia → London ON (~200 mi). Total ~530 km. Border crossing Jul 5 — check CBSA waits; post–Jul 4 weekend can be busy.

recommendations.text: Plan Benton Harbor stop 11 am–1 pm — allow buffer for I-94 traffic out of Milwaukee.
Blue Water Bridge (Port Huron–Sarnia) border wait times — check CBSA app before crossing.
CBSA ArriveCAN / ROVR — confirm current entry requirements before crossing.
Fuel in Port Huron before border — fill tank in USD before switching to CAD prices.
Lake Michigan ferry (Muskegon–Milwaukee) is an alternate scenic route; not used on this day.
511 Ontario for closures.
Stratford or Niagara detour if avoiding 401 stress.
